asset_name: Warrior unit — Raid shield raise sheet
shared_properties: The unit keeps the 32x48px footprint, shield silhouette, and helmet mass established by art_000003.
Core palette fields remain fixed at primary #111111, background #000000, and accent #FFFFFF.
The battle-facing side-view remains aligned with the world grid so combat overlays can reuse placement rules.
differences: The shield lifts higher and the spear angle rises to communicate an active raid-defense state.
The cloak and beard silhouette lean forward to signal impact preparation during hostile event windows.
Frame cadence rises to 10fps so the guard response feels sharper than the idle loop.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the winter survival loop, queue-memory mechanic, building roster, and 1920x1080 Bevy target.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the monochrome blizzard mood, Viking settlement silhouettes, and snow density targets.

reference_images.label: jarl_of_blizzard_village_scene
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the settlement composition, building spacing, and whiteout layering over the frozen basin.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas battlefield unit sprite that replaces the idle guard art during raid-response moments.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load character/art_000004.png with the warrior atlas family and activate it when raid-defense state is entered.
runtime_notes: Trigger this variant during raid warnings and keep the pivot aligned with art_000003 for stable swaps.
Use authored shield highlights instead of runtime flashes so silhouette readability remains deterministic.
Advance the response cycle only while the raid state is active to avoid over-busy village idles.

context.usage: Raised-shield warrior state used when raiders approach and the village defense posture intensifies.

shape.silhouette: Aligned Viking guard with shield lifted to the face line, spear poised upward, and cloak swept forward by impact wind.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 192x48px with 6 frames of 32x48px and 0px spacing.
The pivot alignment matches art_000003 so state swaps do not slide the unit on the tile grid.
The raised shield clearly covers the torso in every frame while the spear tip remains inside the 32x48px bounds.
